An analysis of fictional travel agency visa statuses using Pandas and Matplotlib.

title: Customer Visa Status Analysis
text: This project demonstrates my ability to clean data and generate visualizations using Python. We generate a mock dataset simulating customer visa approvals for a travel agency, and then plot the results.

title: Customer Visa Status Analysis
text: This project demonstrates my ability to clean data and generate visualizations using Python. We generate a mock dataset simulating customer visa approvals for a travel agency, and then plot the results.

title: Customer Visa Status Analysis
text:
Data Generation
We’ll use pandas and matplotlib for this analysis.


Code
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Generate mock data
np.random.seed(42)
categories = ['Approved', 'Pending', 'Rejected', 'More Info Required']
data = np.random.choice(categories, 100, p=[0.6, 0.2, 0.1, 0.1])

df = pd.DataFrame({'Visa_Status': data})
status_counts = df['Visa_Status'].value_counts()

print("Raw Data Sample:")
print(df.head())


Raw Data Sample:
          Visa_Status
0            Approved
1  More Info Required
2             Pending
3            Approved
4            Approved

title: Customer Visa Status Analysis
text:
Visualization
Next, we render a bar chart to visualize the distribution of visa statuses. Quarto automatically executes this code chunk and embeds the resulting plot directly in the output document.


Code
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
status_counts.plot(kind='bar', color=['#27ae60', '#f39c12', '#e74c3c', '#3498db'])
plt.title('Customer Visa Status Overview', fontsize=14)
plt.ylabel('Number of Applicants')
plt.xlabel('Status')
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()








Figure 1: Distribution of Visa Statuses






Observations
As we can see from Figure 1, the vast majority of applications are Approved, which indicates the travel agency has a robust documentation process prior to submission.

title: Reflection
text:
Throughout this module, I have gained substantial confidence in bridging the gap between raw data and actionable insights.

Key Takeaways

Version Control: Getting comfortable with Git and GitHub has dramatically improved my workflow.
Reproducible Research: Using Quarto to blend Python code directly with my reports ensures my analysis is transparent and easily updatable.
Data Visualization: I learned how crucial narrative pacing and visual clarity are when presenting findings to non-technical stakeholders.







Note



Building this very ePortfolio in Quarto was an excellent capstone experience, marrying coding with clean web design.




“Data is like garbage. You’d better know what you are going to do with it before you collect it.” - Mark Twain
